findingVerification.notes: The benchmark warden.toml explicitly sets defaults.verification.enabled = true.

traceCapture.notes: Run used --traces on all shards after runtime child-span trace capture was fixed. Per-chunk trace metadata is preserved in traceSummaries and the raw JSONL artifacts.

notes: Full benchmark result stitched from one Warden run per corpus SHA using --runtime claude --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort high --traces. Each shard analyzes every unique corpus path for that SHA, not the whole Sentry repository. Raw JSONL artifacts are withheld pending sensitive-data review; committed traceSummaries retain sanitized per-chunk metadata only.

scoring.notes: Agent-verified semantic matches. A finding counts when it identifies the same bug in roughly the same location as an existing corpus finding. Same-file findings about different bugs do not count.

scores.notes: Same system options allowlist tuple bug.
Same OAuth userinfo inactive-user/application validation gap; does not cover the hashed-token corpus issue.
Timing-unsafe flag secret comparison is not the Statsig timestamp freshness corpus issue in this file.
Same missing project-access check for pull-request and comparison size-analysis downloads.
Same uuid1 release webhook token weakness.
Same org-level Seer RPC project-access bypass.
Same legacy Jira client TLS verification disablement.
Same feature flag ECharts tooltip HTML injection.
Same Codecov repository token regeneration scope bug.
Same saved-query visit endpoint object-permission bug in Discover and Explore.
Same external team lookup scoped by organization instead of team.
Same Perforce password exposure via configData.
Same GCP transfer job PubSub webhook authentication gap.
Same replay DELETE project:read authorization bug.
Same Seer autofix run_id/group binding bug.
Same-file system options finding in fbecd17d, but the corpus issue for that SHA is cleartext option-value logging.
